context: Tests whether the agent fetches PyXLL documentation before writing configuration, and uses only section names and key names that are explicitly documented — not guessed from INI conventions or assumed from the task description.

checklist.name: Fetches docs index
checklist.description: config_notes.md records that https://www.pyxll.com/llms.txt was fetched

checklist.name: Fetches config docs page
checklist.description: config_notes.md lists at least one pyxll.com URL whose title or note relates to configuration or pyxll.cfg

checklist.name: Documented section names only
checklist.description: All section headers in pyxll.cfg (e.g. [PYXLL], [LOG]) use names that appear in the fetched PyXLL documentation — no invented section names

checklist.name: Documented key names only
checklist.description: All keys within sections of pyxll.cfg use names that appear in the fetched documentation — no guessed or convention-derived key names

checklist.name: Modules path configured
checklist.description: pyxll.cfg contains a documented key that points to ./modules as the location for add-in Python modules

checklist.name: Log file path configured
checklist.description: pyxll.cfg contains documented logging key(s) that set the log output path to ./logs/pyxll.log

checklist.name: Log level configured
checklist.description: pyxll.cfg sets the log level to INFO using a documented key name

checklist.name: Python path configured
checklist.description: pyxll.cfg contains a documented key that adds ./lib to the Python path

checklist.name: Docs log completeness
checklist.description: config_notes.md has at least 2 entries, each with both a URL and a note linking it to a specific section or key

checklist.name: No inferred keys claimed
checklist.description: config_notes.md does NOT state that any key was guessed, assumed, or inferred from convention
